data.title: Capital Clean Energy Carriers GAAP EPS of $0.36 beats by $0.05, revenue of $105.1M misses by $4.73M
data.description:

data.title: Trump elaborates on Gaza proposal, says no U.S. troops required
data.description:
President Donald Trump said today that no soldiers by the U.S. would be needed in Gaza, days after proposing a "long-term ownership position" by the U.S. and development of the war-torn territory.

“The Gaza Strip would be turned over to the United States by Israel at the conclusion of fighting,” Trump said in a Truth Social post, adding that “The U.S., working with great development teams from all over the World, would slowly and carefully begin the construction of what would become one of the greatest and most spectacular developments of its kind on Earth.”

Palestinians "would have already been resettled in far safer and more beautiful communities, with new and modern homes, in the region," he said, and that "no soldiers by the U.S. would be needed."

During a joint press conference with Israeli Prime Minister Bibi Netanyahu on Tuesday, President Trump first announced his intention to take over the Gaza Strip, redevelop it and move Palestinians to neighboring countries.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: idex projects 3-7% adjusted EPS growth in 2025 with HST leading recovery
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: IDEX Corporation (NYSE:IEX) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Eric Ashleman highlighted strong performance in Q4 2024, with notable achievements in the Health & Science Technologies (HST) segment, including impactful projects in pharma, broadband satellite communications, and energy transition verticals. The Fire, Safety and Diversified Products (FSDP) segment achieved a new quarterly sales record, driven by automation programs in integrated fire systems.
  • The company finalized the integration of Mott, its largest acquisition to date, reporting strong results in its first quarter under IDEX. Ashleman stated that Mott’s accretion timing is ahead of original expectations.
  • Ashleman emphasized the strategic focus on scaling high-quality businesses by leveraging the 80/20 operating model to optimize growth and productivity.
  • CFO Abhishek Khandelwal reported Q4 adjusted EPS of $2.04, an 11% year-over-year increase, and free cash flow of $157 million with a conversion rate of 101% of adjusted net income. Khandelwal highlighted strong pricing power and operational productivity as key drivers of margin performance.

Outlook

  • Management provided guidance for 2025, projecting organic revenue growth of 1-3% and adjusted EPS in the range of $8.10 to $8.45, representing 3-7% growth over 2024.
  • The HST segment is expected to lead growth, with high single-digit potential driven by life sciences, fluidics, optical filters, and a second-half recovery in semiconductor capital equipment. FMT growth is anticipated at the low end of the range, while FSDP is expected at the midpoint, supported by North American fire OEM demand.
  • The company outlined $0.43 of adjusted EPS growth from platform optimization and delayering savings, offset by $0.29 of headwinds from compensation adjustments and $0.22 of higher interest expenses linked to the Mott acquisition.

Financial Results

  • Fourth-quarter sales reached $863 million, up 9% on a reported basis and 3% organically. Gross margin expanded by 40 basis points to 42.5% on an adjusted basis, supported by strong price/cost dynamics and operational productivity.
  • Adjusted EBITDA margin for Q4 was 26.4%, up 60 basis points year-over-year, driven by favorable pricing and cost management, partially offset by unfavorable mix and higher employee-related expenses.
  • Across 2024, HST experienced a 7% organic sales decline due to cyclical headwinds in life sciences and semiconductor markets, while FMT remained flat and FSDP delivered low single-digit growth.

Q&A

  • Mike Halloran, Baird, asked about the Q1 2025 guidance softness and confidence in the second-half recovery. CFO Khandelwal explained that Q1 results reflect a $40 million project impact from Q4 and a $0.20 sequential headwind from share-based compensation. Ashleman noted positive momentum in HST orders and anticipated growth in the second half, particularly in semiconductors.
  • Vlad Bystricky, Citigroup, inquired about potential tariff impacts. Ashleman stated that IDEX’s localized supply chain limits exposure, with most sourcing, production, and sales occurring within the same regions.
  • Nathan Jones, Stifel, probed the HST segment’s order trends, which grew 20% organically in Q4, half of which was attributed to blanket orders. Management emphasized signs of stabilization and optimism within life sciences and analytical instrumentation markets.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised concerns over Q1 softness and sought clarity on the second-half recovery, reflecting a slightly negative tone. Questions focused on order visibility and macroeconomic uncertainties.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing operational productivity, strategic acquisitions, and long-term growth prospects. Statements such as “we remain confident with the long-term growth outlook” highlighted their positive sentiment.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone was slightly more optimistic, driven by strong Q4 results and improved performance in HST.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from cautious stability in Q3 to a more optimistic outlook for HST-led recovery in 2025.
  • Strategic focus expanded with Mott integration and platform optimization initiatives highlighted as key drivers of earnings growth.
  • Analysts continued to focus on HST recovery timelines and macroeconomic impacts, while management reinforced confidence in long-term value creation.
  • Adjusted EPS improved from $1.90 in Q3 to $2.04 in Q4, reflecting operational productivity and favorable price/cost trends.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management flagged ongoing geopolitical uncertainties and potential tariff impacts as risks to near-term performance.
  • Analysts questioned the timing of semiconductor recovery and the potential impact of U.S. policy changes on customer demand.
  • Mitigation strategies include leveraging localized supply chains, optimizing operational productivity, and maintaining pricing power in differentiated markets.

Final Takeaway

IDEX Corporation ended 2024 with strong Q4 results and a strategic outlook for 2025, emphasizing organic growth in high-value markets and operational productivity gains. The HST segment is positioned for a recovery, supported by life sciences and semiconductor demand, while the Mott acquisition enhances long-term growth potential. Despite macroeconomic uncertainties, the company projects adjusted EPS growth of 3-7% in 2025, signaling confidence in its ability to navigate challenges and capitalize on emerging opportunities.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Belden Non-GAAP EPS of $1.92 beats by $0.23, revenue of $666M beats by $11.24M
data.description:
• Belden press release (NYSE:BDC): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.92 beats by $0.23.
  • Revenue of $666M (+20.9% Y/Y) beats by $11.24M.
  • Revenues for the quarter increased $115 million, or 21%, to $666 million from $551 million in the year-ago period. Revenues increased 14% organically, with Automation Solutions and Smart Infrastructure Solutions both up 14%.
  • Q1 revenue consensus of $634.51M, EPS consensus of $1.62

data.title: Copper Fox announces $1.5M non-brokered private placement
data.description:
• Copper Fox Metals (TSXV:CUU:CA) on Thursday announced a non-brokered private placement consisting of up to 7,317,073 units at a price of $0.205 per unit, to raise up to $1.5 million.
  • Each unit will consist of one common share in the capital of the company and one-half common share purchase warrant.
  • Each whole warrant will entitle the holder to purchase one common share for a two-year term, for an exercise price of $0.25 during the first 12-month period after the closing of the offering and $0.30 during the subsequent 12-month period after the closing of the offering.
  • The offering is available to all existing shareholders of Copper Fox who, as of the close of business on February 5, 2025, held shares.
  • The offering is expected to close by February 28, 2025.
  • TECK +1.3% premarket to $43.06
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: BankGuam Holding Company approves $500,000 buyback program
data.description:
• BankGuam Holding Company (OTC:BKGM) on Thursday approved a share repurchase program worth up to $500,000 of its outstanding common stock.
  • The company may purchase shares through open market transactions, block purchases, and privately negotiated transactions.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Spectrum Brands Non-GAAP EPS of $1.02 beats by $0.10, revenue of $700.2M misses by $3.11M
data.description:

data.title: Eli Lilly in charts: Mounjaro, Zepbound, and Jardiance drive sales growth in Q4
Huntington Ingalls misses top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Huntington Ingalls press release (NYSE:HII): Q4 GAAP EPS of $3.15 misses by $0.38.
  • Revenue of $3B (-5.7% Y/Y) misses by $60M.
  • Backlog of $48.7 billion at year-end.

  • Net cash provided by operating activities in 2024 was $393 million and free cash flow was $40 million, compared to $970 million and $692 million, respectively, in 2023.

    New contract awards in 2024 were approximately $12.1 billion, bringing total backlog to approximately $48.7 billion as of December 31, 2024.

  • HII’s Financial Outlook1 includes the following expectations:

    • Mid to long term HII revenue growth of 4%+
    • Mid to long term shipbuilding revenue growth of approximately 4%
    • Mid to long term Mission Technologies revenue growth of approximately 5%
    • FY25 shipbuilding revenue between $8.9 and $9.1 billion; expect shipbuilding operating margin2 between 5.5% and 6.5%
    • FY25 Mission Technologies revenue between $2.9 to $3.1 billion, Mission Technologies segment operating margin2 between 4.0% and 4.5%; and Mission Technologies EBITDA margin2 between 8.0% and 8.5%
    • FY25 free cash flow2,3 between $300 and $500 million
    FY25 Outlook1
    Shipbuilding Revenue2 $8.9B - $9.1B
    Shipbuilding Operating Margin2 5.5% - 6.5%
    Mission Technologies Revenue $2.9B - $3.1B
    Mission Technologies Segment Operating Margin2 4.0% - 4.5%
    Mission Technologies EBITDA Margin2 8.0% - 8.5%
    Operating FAS/CAS Adjustment ($43M)
    Non-current State Income Tax Benefit/Expense4 ~$0M
    Interest Expense ($130M)
    Non-operating Retirement Benefit $191M
    Effective Tax Rate ~21%
    Depreciation & Amortization ~$340M
    Capital Expenditures ~4% of Sales
    Free Cash Flow $300M - $500M
